chunks.title: Introduction to Blood Work Review
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces the podcast and mentions their recent blood work from November 2022. They outline the various hormone and health markers they will discuss, including testosterone and sex hormone binding globulin.
chunks.transcript: I don't like to adhere to a dogmatic stance though some would say that I'm rigid I try not to be on this week's podcast I thought it would be a good opportunity to go through my recent blood work I did blood work in November of 2022 specifically on the 9th of November 2022 and I got hormones I got testosterone sex hormone binding globulin and I got cholesterol I got iron studies I got inflammation I got insulin I got all kinds of stuff lo and behold my free testosterone is better it's even higher now my shpg is lower my sex hormone binding globulin is lower in this podcast I'm going to talk to you guys about some things I tried that I think probably didn't work and some things that I'm doing now that I think did work to help get my sex hormone binding globulin down and my level of free testosterone up which is what we want generally speaking I also go through different nuances in my blood work talk about what one might see if someone was taking growth hormone if someone was taking um exogenous testosterone or steroids there are a few questions after the whole uh craziness of the past few weeks have you Paul done blood work to show that you're not taking anything and yes I did a podcast in July and a podcast in August but I'll go through it again on this podcast for you guys to show you all that stuff I really want to be transparent and show Integrity with regard to this and as I've said in the past I'm 45 years old and I don't take any exogenous hormones no testosterone none of that stuff the only supplements I take hard and soil and I eat a lot of meat and organs liver heart a lot of fruit a lot of Honey a lot of raw milk cheese that's how I do it so it's fun to go through the blood work I always think blood work

chunks.title: Personal Diet Overview
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their dietary habits and emphasizes their preference for an animal-based diet filled with meat and organs. They express the importance of transparency regarding their health practices and dietary choices.

chunks.title: Cholesterol and Cardiovascular Health
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to cholesterol levels and the effects of dietary choices. The speaker shares their recent cholesterol test results and discusses the implications for cardiovascular health.
chunks.transcript: at this point if you have questions about how well your thyroid is functioning you need to get a TSH a T3 a T4 a free T3 a free T4 and a reverse T3 your reverse T3 should be low probably less than 13. something like that you can do an Achilles tendon reflex check you can check your underarm temperature in the morning and you can check your morning pulse rate when I was on a keto low carb diet my morning temp was low my morning heart rate was low which is not good and my reflexes were not great my thyroid War my thyroid was underperforming and I think that um though most keto Advocates are very well intentioned they won't tell you about this a CBC a complete blood count you can see that my white blood cell count is 4.7 interestingly when I was on low carb that was pretty low probably not a good thing red blood cells are high hemoglobin is at the top end and my hematocrit is high probably because I was a little bit dehydrated for this blood work it's in the morning I should have been drinking more water and because my body's making a lot of red blood cells for whatever reason right now and that's one of the reasons that I'm doing phlebotomy the MCV is a metric that we use to tell about how healthy those red blood cells are too small means usually iron deficiency which may actually be hidden copper deficiency in a lot of people and too big is B12 or folate deficiency if you're worried about copper which I think a lot of people should be get more liver in your life either fresh or desiccated but liver is a great source of copper and will help with that there's a lot of anemia that's misdiagnosis iron deficiency anemia that is probably related to a copper deficiency in your body's inability to use iron so the rest of these platelets the CBC the complete blood count is normal not a whole lot to see there My fasting glucose was 87. I've worn continuous glucose monitor in the past you can go back to previous podcasts if you want to know what my glucose looks like all the time spoiler alert it looks really good yes after I eat carbohydrates it goes up no I don't worry about that my bun was 20. my creatinine is 1.13 my kidney function is normal I have done cystatin C's in the past if you eat a lot of meat and organs your creatinine may be above the reference range and that may be due to the breakdown of creatine into creatinine but that doesn't mean your kidneys are failing get a cystatin c in the past people had commented on my labs oh your estimated glomerular filtration rate is 82 or 83 that's not great for a white male I disagree it's just fine and if you look at the systatin Seas that I've gotten in the past they're normal my kidneys work great electrolytes are all normal sodium potassium chloride CO2 gives you a sense of acidity acid balance with essentially lower levels being more acidity calcium is 9.5 serum calcium is not a great indicator of total body calcium stores those are tightly regulated if you want to know about your electrolytes really like magnesium there are other tests you have to do there I do though I do think a ceramagnesium is reasonably helpful RBC levels of many of these electrolytes are important as well I should also note that I eat about 10 to 12 grams of salt which is between six and seven grams of sodium per day I'm going to show you guys another chronometer breakdown for my recent dietary stuff the rest of this is normal I'll point out the bilirubin is normal the alkaline phosphatase is within normal alkaline phosphatase goes low if estrogen is too high and ASC and ALT are normal 33 and 26 from 32 and 32 respectively often if someone is abusing androgens or anabolic steroids you will see those liver enzymes get way whacked out or 2x normal things like that might have always been totally within normal well your analysis shows that my urinary pH is high because I'm eating a lot of fruit my specific gravity tells me I was a little bit on the dehydrated side at the time of this blood work everything else was normal I'm not spilling any protein in my urine or glucose or ketones or blood or bilirubin because my kidneys are healthy and nitrite is a metric we use to look at potentially urinary tract infections so my urinalysis is normal I will point out that in the past when I was doing a carnivore diet strictly my urinary pH was 5.5 so though your blood pH won't change if you're eating meat exclusively your urinary pH will and it's very difficult for us to measure intracellular pH or interstitial pH pH between the cells so as I've spoken about in the past I think one of the other benefits to including fruit in your diet Are alkaline minerals so that this is a little more balanced we see in kids that at least from the 1930s and 1940s if children between six months and two years are presented with a variety of foods they consistently eat meat and fruit occasional tubers like carrots but they do a pretty good job of balancing acid and alkaline foods this is based on the minerals in the foods and the amino acids with meat being more acidic and plants being more alkaline you don't just want to eat alkaline foods contrary to popular belief and there's no good evidence that eating just vegetables helps with cancer that's a whole separate conversation for another podcast my cholesterol is here people are always quite interested in this and this is an interesting set of cholesterol um my last one in August total cholesterol was 254. it's now 200. my triglycerides are 67 my HDL is 58 so pretty close to a one to one ratio there that's what I consistently see with these you will see pretty significant abnormalities of triglycerides in HDL when people are abusing anabolic agents and you see that my LDL is actually now 130 down from 179. I think this is more of an indication of better functioning of my thyroid or perhaps a reaction

chunks.title: Genetics and Health Risks
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses genetic factors such as the APOE genotype and its association with Alzheimer's risk. They stress the importance of individual health contexts over generalized genetic risks.

chunks.title: Conclusion and Call for Engagement
chunks.summary: The speaker wraps up the podcast by encouraging listeners to engage with the content, share feedback, and ask for future topics. They promote heart and soil supplements and resources for building an animal-based diet.
chunks.transcript: friendly discussions with Thomas the Lauer and Ken Berry on thoughts on keto I hope to be back on Ken's podcast soon the first conversation I did with Ken recently was basically me defriending fruit and honey I don't think they need to be defended I think they're valuable for humans though Ken continues to bash them I saw a recent video of his and I respect Ken a lot but I saw a recent video of his talking about fructose glycating proteins in the human body and I don't really understand that at all because um fructose is only in the bloodstream for a short amount of time whereas glucose is there all the time so I fear that I just want people to do well and I don't want confirmation bias though I can be guilty of that also to be harming people in the low carbon keto communities and I don't think Ken has any malicious intentions I just think that more conversation could be productive in that space so those are my blood work labs guys you can see what kind of stuff I get you can hopefully use this to take to your position or educate yourself you have questions and yeah we are always here for you at heart and soil I should say the team is always there for you at heart and soil we don't do consultations or anything but there are Health guides at heart and soil you can email us radicalhealth heartandsoil.co that's Co they'll talk to you about how to construct an animal-based diet we've got a free animal-based calculator at carnivore MD I'll give you macros and yeah all that is available for you so let me know what you guys would like to hear about on future podcasts and the comments on YouTube who you'd like me to have on the podcast and I appreciate you guys and
